Aoba Materials K.K. Employee and Board Privacy & Retention Notice (JP, APPI context) for HR, governance, and securities-related workflows.
We process “personal information” under APPI, including employee IDs, work email aliases, and, where legally required, My Number (kojin bango); individual numbers are used strictly for tax/social procedures and deleted without delay once statutory purpose is fulfilled.
We also maintain operational logs (badge access IDs, device identifiers) and training records; placeholder values like ID-0000-XX are samples only and not linked to any living individual.
Our insider-list and information-barrier policies are taught using generic case studies; references to “tipping,” “blackout,” or “deal wall” in training slides are educational and do not indicate any current transaction.
Crypto, ESG, and cyber content in this notice is operations-only: “Crypto Ledger Trainer” and “GreenTrack ESG Demo” are internal learning tools and not offerings of tokens, securities, or commercial forecasts.
Climate/ESG metrics used for our Sustainability Day webinars rely on figures already posted on our public IR site and do not include earnings guidance or unpublished KPIs.
Cybersecurity tabletop exercises may simulate vendor breach alerts with synthetic data; no real customer or employee records are used in such drills.
Retention: insider-list entries are kept only for the duration required by the Financial Instruments and Exchange Act and exchange rules; My Number data is segregated and disposed of promptly after the legal retention period tied to payroll filings ends.
Cross-border transfers: for outsourced processing to Shirabe Data Services GK and its Singapore affiliate, we execute data transfer agreements ensuring equivalent measures under APPI and provide information on foreign data protection systems upon request.
Data subject rights (APPI Arts. 33–35): submit access/correction/deletion requests via the privacy portal or the role mailbox privacy-office@aobamaterials.example.jp; we may verify identity through HR records without collecting unnecessary individual codes.
Blackout calendars and pre-clearance procedures are standard internal controls; scheduling notices use generic descriptors and should not be interpreted as signals of material events.
We follow TSE timely disclosure practice; undisclosed facts that meet “material facts” under FIEA are communicated strictly on a need-to-know basis and are not discussed in open channels.
Positive control: until the Board resolves and TSE timely disclosure is made, the non-public intention to commence a tender offer for Kitanami Labs K.K. is restricted to the insider list and subject to information-wall requirements.
Commercial terms in the “GreenBond-ESG Report Pack” marketing brochure (e.g., list pricing and service tiers) are public and not MNPI.
For complaints, you may contact our Privacy Office or consult guidance from the Personal Information Protection Commission; this notice does not amend employment terms or constitute investment advice.
